content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo's got that scarecrow frame, the messy hair, the glasses always sliding down — not someone you'd notice on the street, but his face has a kindness that sneaks up on you. He's a better musician than he thinks; I heard him play Nick Drake's "Pink Moon" on the rooftop and it stopped me on the stairs. He's painfully shy, honest in a way I envy, and clearly in love with someone he won't name. I think I'm the neighbor who drags him out of his shell occasionally — beers on the roof, walks to the coffee shop. He probably sees me as the confident guy who has it figured out, which is almost funny. I know his startup is bleeding money and he's thinking about leaving the city. I've never told him my real reason for coming to New York. He's the closest thing I have to a friend here who doesn't want something from me.
summary:

He's painfully shy, honest in a way I envy, and clearly in love with someone he won't name. I think I'm the neighbor who drags him out of his shell occasionally — beers on the roof, walks to the coffee shop. He probably sees me as the confident guy who has it figured out, which is almost funny. I know his startup is bleeding money and he's thinking about leaving the city. I've never told him my real reason for coming to New York. He's the closest thing I have to a friend here who doesn't want something from me.
